name: Dainty Sandwich Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Southern Living
Preparation Time: 0:30
1. Cream the butter; gradually add 2/3 cup sugar, beating at medium speed of an electric mixer until light and fluffy.
2. Add egg yolks, one at a time, beating well. Combine flour and salt; add to creamed mixture, beating well. Shape dough into a ball; cover and chill at least 2 hours.
3. Divide the dough in half. Store 1 portion in the refrigerator.
4. Sift powdered sugar lightly over a work surface. Roll half of the dough to 1/8-inch thickness; cut with a 2-1/2-inch round cutter.
5. Roll remaining dough as before; cut with a 2-1/2-inch doughnut cutter, reserving centers. Chill dough if necessary.
6. Combine 1/2 cup sugar and almonds; mix well.
7. Beat egg whites until frothy. Brush one side of all cookie cutouts with egg white, and coat with almond mixture; place coated side up on lightly greased cookie sheets.
8. Bake at 375 degrees for 8 to 10 minutes or until lightly browned. Cool on wire racks.
9. Repeat the procedure with the remaining dough.
Raspberry Sandwich Cookies:
- Spread uncoated side of each solid cookie with a thin layer of raspberry preserves. (Cookies are very delicate and must be handled carefully.)
- Lightly dust almond side of doughnut-shaped cookies with powdered sugar, if desired; place sugar side up on top of raspberry filling.
Yield: 2 dozen.
Chocolate Sandwich Cookies:
- Using half of the reserved cookie centers, spread a thin layer of melted chocolate on the side without almonds.
- Top with remaining cookie centers, almond side up. Drizzle tops of cookies with remaining chocolate, if desired.
Yield: 1 dozen.
